# Agent Use Interface (AUI) — Authoring Reference

> Use this document to create valid AUI XML files. AUI describes URL-parameter-driven tasks (searches, filters, share intents, config links) as an XML catalog served at `/.well-known/aui.xml`.

## XML Boilerplate

Use this minimal required boilerplate:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<aui xmlns="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1" version="0.1">
  ...
</aui>
```

Optional but recommended for validator/tooling compatibility:

```xml
<aui
  xmlns="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1"
  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
  xsi:schemaLocation="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1 https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1/aui.xsd"
  version="0.1">
```

Optional for browser readability (must be the first line after the XML declaration):

```xml
<?xml-stylesheet type="text/css" href="aui.css"?>
```

## Element Reference

### `<aui>` (root)

Attributes:
- `version` (required): Must be `"0.1"`.

Children (all required unless noted):
- `<name>`: Human-readable service name.
- `<origin>`: Canonical origin (scheme + host, e.g., `https://shop.example.com`). Must be HTTPS. No trailing slash, no path.
- `<description>`: What this service does, written for agent comprehension.
- `<metadata>` (optional): See below.
- `<tasks>`: Container for `<task>` elements.

### `<metadata>` (optional)

All children optional:
- `<logo>`: URL to a logo image.
- `<contact>`: Contact email.
- `<docs>`: URL to human-readable documentation.
- `<platforms>`: Container for `<platform>` elements. Values: `ios`, `android`, `web`.

### `<task>`

Two forms: **inline** (full definition) or **reference** (summary + link to detail file).

Attributes:
- `id` (required): Unique kebab-case identifier. Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
- `output` (optional, inline only): `display` (default) or `background`. Omit when `href` is present.
- `href` (optional): URL to a detail file. When present, task is in reference form.

Children:
- `<name>` (required): Short name.
- `<description>` (required): What this task does, written for agent comprehension. Describe when to use it and what outcome to expect.
- `<base-path>` (required for inline, omit for reference): URL path. Must start with `/`, no `?` or `#`. Final URL = `{origin}{base-path}?{params}`.
- `<tags>` (optional): Container for `<tag>` elements (freeform categorization strings).
- `<parameters>` (required for inline, omit for reference): Container for `<param>` elements.
- `<examples>` (optional for inline, omit for reference): Container for `<example>` elements.

### `<param>`

Attributes:
- `name` (required): Query parameter key as it appears in the URL. Pattern: `[A-Za-z0-9._~-]+`
- `type` (required): `string`, `number`, `integer`, `boolean`, or `enum`.
- `required` (optional): `true` or `false` (default `false`).

Children:
- `<description>` (required): What this parameter represents.
- `<default>` (optional): Default value if omitted.
- `<pattern>` (optional): ECMAScript regex the value must match (no delimiters).
- `<min>` (optional): Minimum value (for `number`/`integer`).
- `<max>` (optional): Maximum value (for `number`/`integer`).
- `<separator>` (optional): Delimiter for multi-value params (e.g., `,`).
- `<example>` (optional): Example value.
- `<options>` (required when `type="enum"`): Container for `<option>` elements.

### `<option>`

Attributes:
- `value` (required): The literal value used in the URL.

Text content: Description of when to use this value.

### `<example>` (task-level)

Children:
- `<intent>` (required): What the user wants, in natural language.
- `<url>` (required): The fully constructed URL. Use `&amp;` for `&` in XML.

### `<aui-task>` (detail file root)

Used as root element when a task is split into a separate file via `href`. Structurally identical to an inline `<task>` but as a standalone document.

Attributes:
- `id` (required): Must match the `id` in the catalog's `<task>` entry.
- `output` (optional): `display` or `background`.

Children: Same as inline `<task>` — `<name>`, `<description>`, `<base-path>`, `<tags>`, `<parameters>`, `<examples>`.

Detail file boilerplate:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<aui-task xmlns="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1"
  id="task-id" output="display">
  ...
</aui-task>
```

## Validation Rules

- Inline tasks (no `href`): `<base-path>` and `<parameters>` are required.
- Reference tasks (has `href`): `<base-path>`, `<parameters>`, `<examples>`, and `output` must be omitted.
- `<param type="enum">` must include `<options>`.
- Detail file `id` must match the catalog `<task>` `id`.
- `<origin>` must match pattern: `https://[^/?#]+(:[0-9]+)?`
- `<base-path>` must match pattern: `/[^?#]*`
- Task `id` must match pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`

## Complete Example

### Catalog (aui.xml)

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<?xml-stylesheet type="text/css" href="aui.css"?>
<aui
  xmlns="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1"
  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
  xsi:schemaLocation="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1 https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1/aui.xsd"
  version="0.1">

  <name>Example Shop</name>
  <origin>https://shop.example.com</origin>
  <description>
    An online store for electronics and accessories. Use these tasks to help
    users search for products, apply filters, share items, and configure wishlists.
  </description>

  <metadata>
    <platforms>
      <platform>ios</platform>
      <platform>android</platform>
      <platform>web</platform>
    </platforms>
    <contact>aui@example.com</contact>
    <docs>https://docs.example.com/universal-links</docs>
  </metadata>

  <tasks>

    <!-- Inline task: full definition here -->
    <task id="product-search">
      <name>Search Products</name>
      <description>
        Search the product catalog. Use this when a user wants to find, browse,
        or discover products. The URL opens the search results page with filters
        pre-applied. On iOS and Android, this opens the native app search experience.
      </description>
      <base-path>/search</base-path>
      <tags>
        <tag>search</tag>
        <tag>discovery</tag>
      </tags>

      <parameters>
        <param name="q" type="string" required="true">
          <description>
            The search query. Should reflect the user's intent in natural
            product-search terms (e.g., 'wireless earbuds noise cancelling').
          </description>
          <example>wireless earbuds</example>
        </param>

        <param name="category" type="enum">
          <description>Filter results to a specific product category.</description>
          <options>
            <option value="electronics">Phones, tablets, laptops, and other devices.</option>
            <option value="accessories">Cases, cables, chargers, and add-ons.</option>
            <option value="audio">Headphones, earbuds, speakers, and microphones.</option>
            <option value="wearables">Smartwatches, fitness trackers, and AR glasses.</option>
            <option value="home">Smart home devices, appliances, and IoT.</option>
          </options>
        </param>

        <param name="price_min" type="number">
          <description>Minimum price in USD.</description>
          <min>0</min>
        </param>

        <param name="price_max" type="number">
          <description>Maximum price in USD.</description>
          <min>0</min>
        </param>

        <param name="sort" type="enum">
          <description>How to sort results.</description>
          <default>relevance</default>
          <options>
            <option value="relevance">Best match for the query (default).</option>
            <option value="price_asc">Cheapest first.</option>
            <option value="price_desc">Most expensive first.</option>
            <option value="rating">Highest rated first.</option>
            <option value="newest">Most recently added.</option>
          </options>
        </param>

        <param name="in_stock" type="boolean">
          <description>Only show items currently in stock.</description>
          <default>true</default>
        </param>
      </parameters>

      <examples>
        <example>
          <intent>Find me noise cancelling headphones under $200</intent>
          <url>https://shop.example.com/search?q=noise+cancelling+headphones&amp;category=audio&amp;price_max=200&amp;sort=rating&amp;in_stock=true</url>
        </example>
        <example>
          <intent>What's new in smart home devices?</intent>
          <url>https://shop.example.com/search?q=smart+home&amp;category=home&amp;sort=newest&amp;in_stock=true</url>
        </example>
      </examples>
    </task>

    <!-- Inline task with pattern validation -->
    <task id="share-product">
      <name>Share Product</name>
      <description>
        Generate a shareable link to a specific product. Use this when a user
        wants to send a product to someone or save it for later.
      </description>
      <base-path>/product</base-path>
      <tags>
        <tag>share</tag>
      </tags>

      <parameters>
        <param name="sku" type="string" required="true">
          <description>The product SKU.</description>
          <pattern>^[A-Z0-9\-]{6,20}$</pattern>
          <example>WH-1000XM5</example>
        </param>

        <param name="ref" type="string">
          <description>Attribution source.</description>
          <default>agent</default>
        </param>
      </parameters>

      <examples>
        <example>
          <intent>Send me a link to the Sony WH-1000XM5</intent>
          <url>https://shop.example.com/product?sku=WH-1000XM5&amp;ref=agent</url>
        </example>
      </examples>
    </task>

    <!-- Reference task: details in a separate file -->
    <task id="configure-wishlist" href="tasks/configure-wishlist.xml">
      <name>Configure Wishlist View</name>
      <description>
        Open the user's wishlist with specific display settings.
      </description>
      <tags>
        <tag>personalization</tag>
      </tags>
    </task>

  </tasks>
</aui>
```

### Detail File (tasks/configure-wishlist.xml)

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<aui-task xmlns="https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1"
  id="configure-wishlist" output="display">

  <name>Configure Wishlist View</name>
  <description>
    Open the user's wishlist with specific display settings.
  </description>
  <base-path>/wishlist</base-path>
  <tags>
    <tag>personalization</tag>
  </tags>

  <parameters>
    <param name="sort" type="enum">
      <description>How to sort wishlist items.</description>
      <default>date_added</default>
      <options>
        <option value="date_added">Most recently saved first (default).</option>
        <option value="price_asc">Cheapest first.</option>
        <option value="price_desc">Most expensive first.</option>
        <option value="price_drop">Largest price drop first.</option>
      </options>
    </param>

    <param name="notify_drops" type="boolean">
      <description>Enable price drop notifications for all wishlist items.</description>
      <default>false</default>
    </param>
  </parameters>

  <examples>
    <example>
      <intent>Show me my wishlist sorted by biggest price drops</intent>
      <url>https://shop.example.com/wishlist?sort=price_drop&amp;notify_drops=true</url>
    </example>
  </examples>
</aui-task>
```

## XSD

Use canonical schema assets (do not rely on an inlined copy):

- Namespace: `https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1`
- XSD (normative): `https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1/aui.xsd`
- Schematron (semantic rules): `https://agentuseinterface.org/schema/0.1/aui.sch`
